race.name: Planica Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers on a World Championship course beneath the Julian Alps — Slovenia's newest marathon where ski jumping history meets cross-country ambition.

vitals.aid_stations: 4 feed stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: Late January in the Planica valley at 850m altitude brings reliable cold and good snow cover. Temperatures range from -10°C to 0°C. The valley location beneath the Julian Alps provides some wind shelter. Snow is generally reliable thanks to the altitude and north-facing aspect.
challenges: January thaw risk at moderate 850m altitude
Valley fog can settle in the Planica basin
Wind channeling through the narrow valley
Variable snow conditions between shaded and exposed sections

course.primary: A relatively flat multi-loop course based at the Planica Nordic Centre, venue of the 2023 FIS Nordic World Ski Championships. The terrain is gentle and beginner-friendly, making it accessible to recreational skiers. Set beneath the dramatic Julian Alps with views of the famous Planica ski jumping hills.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks on World Championship-grade venue
course.grooming: World Championship venue standard — excellent machine grooming
features: Raced on the 2023 FIS Nordic World Ski Championships course
Views of the iconic Planica ski jumping hills
Flat, beginner-friendly course accessible to all levels

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: World Championship venue provides excellent infrastructure, grooming, and organization for a young event. Julian Alps scenery and Euroloppet membership from 2025 signal growth potential. Low prestige score reflects the event's youth — founded only in 2020.

history.summary: The Planica Ski Marathon is one of the newest events on the European marathon circuit, launched in 2020 at the Planica Nordic Centre in Slovenia's Julian Alps. The venue gained global attention as host of the 2023 FIS Nordic World Ski Championships. Joining the Euroloppet series in 2025 marks a significant milestone, bringing international marathon skiers to a venue already renowned for ski jumping and World Cup cross-country racing.
notable_facts: Founded in 2020 — one of the newest Euroloppet events
Raced at the venue of the 2023 FIS Nordic World Ski Championships
Planica is historically famous for ski jumping — first 100m+ jump in 1936
Slovenia's growing presence on the international marathon skiing calendar
Flat course design makes it one of the most beginner-friendly Euroloppet events
